{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cm.3","title":"Historical events system: Black Plague and Renaissance","description":"Implement named historical events that fire based on age/tech milestones. Black Plague is a guaranteed mega-pandemic in Medieval Age (-50% work rate, 15% death chance, 90s). Renaissance is a per-player golden age triggered by Printing Press + Banking + Guilds (+35% research, +50% Knowledge, 3 min). Phoenix interaction: Renaissance within 120s of plague gives 1.5x bonuses. Settings in data/settings/historical_events.json.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Black Plague fires once per game in Medieval Age (random delay 180-360s into age)
- [ ] Black Plague affects all players simultaneously
- [ ] Black Plague effects: -50% work rate, 15% villager death chance, -75% trade income, 90s duration
- [ ] Medical tech mitigations reduce Black Plague severity (Herbalism, Sanitation, Vaccines=immune)
- [ ] Post-plague aftermath bonuses: +15% work rate, +20% research speed for 120s
- [ ] Renaissance triggers per-player when Printing Press + Banking + Guilds all researched
- [ ] Renaissance effects: +35% research, +50% Knowledge gen, +20% gold, 180s duration
- [ ] Bonus effects for 3+ Libraries and 2+ Markets during Renaissance
- [ ] Phoenix interaction: Renaissance within 120s of plague survival → 1.5x multiplier
- [ ] Visual effects: dark fog (plague), golden glow (renaissance)
- [ ] Audio: death knells (plague), fanfare (renaissance)
- [ ] Announcement text for both events
- [ ] Unit tests for trigger conditions, effect application, mitigation stacking
- [ ] Integration test: advance to Medieval → plague fires → research 3 techs → renaissance triggers","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:20Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:20Z","labels":["events","historical","story"]}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each pirate assigned random bounty on spawn (min_gold to max_gold)
- [ ] Bounty scaled by age multiplier from settings

- [ ] Bounty amount shown in pirate info panel when selected

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc","title":"Epic: Codebase Health — Duplication, Performance \u0026 Architecture","description":"Address code duplication, performance bottlenecks, and architectural issues identified in comprehensive codebase review. Covers shared utility extraction, spatial indexing, god object decomposition, event-driven rendering, and organizational cleanup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:03Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:03Z","labels":["architecture","epic","performance","refactor"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.1","title":"Extract shared utilities for _get_game_delta() and _dl_settings()","description":"Extract _get_game_delta() (duplicated in 8 files: wolf_ai, pirate_ai, dog_ai, historical_event_manager, pirate_manager, trade_cart_ai, prototype_resource_node, pandemic_manager) and _dl_settings()/_load_config() (duplicated in 40+ files) into a shared utility module. Create a GameUtils or NodeUtils autoload/static class. Eliminates ~200+ lines of copy-paste code.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:18Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:18Z","labels":["duplication","refactor","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.3","title":"Make minimap redraw event-driven with dirty flag","description":"minimap.gd calls queue_redraw() unconditionally in _process() every frame, triggering ~80,000 set_pixel operations at 60fps (two 200x200 images). Replace with a dirty flag that only triggers redraw when visibility or fog state actually changes. Connect to visibility_manager signals instead of polling.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:20Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:20Z","labels":["performance","rendering","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.9","title":"Introduce event bus for cross-system communication","description":"Currently prototype_main.gd has hard references to 28 systems, and ResourceManager.add_resource() is called directly by units, buildings, tech, trade, and civs. Changes to shared interfaces ripple across 40+ files. Bidirectional coupling exists between TechManager↔HistoricalEventManager and PopulationManager↔PandemicManager. Introduce an EventBus singleton to decouple systems: resource changes, combat events, tech completions, and victory conditions flow through the bus. Add event logging for debugging. This also provides a natural point for event ordering guarantees (currently signal handler execution order is undefined).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:45Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:45Z","labels":["architecture","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.15","title":"Reduce combat stats dictionary allocations","description":"prototype_unit.gd _deal_damage_to_target() creates two new dictionaries (_build_stats_dict and _build_target_stats_dict) on every attack (every 0.5-2s per unit). With 20+ active combats, this creates GC pressure. Reuse dictionaries with clear() + reset, or pass pre-allocated stat references instead of creating new dicts each time.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:08Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:08Z","labels":["performance","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.7","title":"Split prototype_main.gd into bootstrapper and flow controller","description":"prototype_main.gd is 1,199 lines — a god object with 64 preloaded scripts, 28 manager references, ~150 lines of setup code. Handles scene assembly, game state transitions, all child system setup, pause/resume, input interception, and victory/defeat flow. Extract: GameBootstrapper (one-time setup, manager initialization, scene assembly), GameFlowController (civ selection → game start → pause → end state machine). Push manager setup into factory pattern. Reduces coupling and makes the game flow testable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:43Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:32:43Z","labels":["architecture","refactor","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.24","title":"Optimize visibility manager FOV computation","description":"visibility_manager.gd update_visibility() runs at ~5Hz and for each player unit (30+), computes shadowcasting FOV — creating new dictionaries and merging them repeatedly. Optimizations: (1) cache FOV shapes per unit type (units of same type at same position have identical FOV), (2) only recompute for units that moved since last update, (3) consider simpler circular FOV for most units, reserving shadowcasting for buildings/towers.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:45Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:45Z","labels":["performance","story"]}

Some buildings have inconsistent optional fields (dock, market, factory, agi_core, wonder missing expected fields). Ensure all building JSON files have a uniform set of fields (even if null/0 for unused ones). Validate against the building schema in data/schemas/. Add schema validation to CI if not already present.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:34Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:34Z","labels":["data","quality","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.13","title":"Replace polling with signals in resource bar and cursor overlay","description":"resource_bar.gd _process() calls _update_transit_labels() every frame, querying river_transport.get_in_transit_resources() and iterating 5 resource types. cursor_overlay.gd updates position every frame even when mouse hasn't moved. Replace with signal-driven updates: resource bar listens for transit_changed signal; cursor overlay only updates on InputEventMouseMotion.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:07Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:07Z","labels":["performance","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.18","title":"Extract command handlers from prototype_input.gd","description":"prototype_input.gd (612 lines) _issue_context_command() has 10+ if/elif branches for different command types (feed, build, gather, garrison, embark, etc). Extract a CommandHandler interface with pluggable handlers for each command type. This reduces prototype_input.gd's size and makes it easier to add new commands without modifying the central dispatcher.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:19Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:19Z","labels":["architecture","refactor","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6cc.16","title":"Optimize formation slot assignment from O(n²) to O(n log n)","description":"prototype_input.gd _assign_slots() is O(n²): for each of n selected units, it iterates all n slot positions to find minimum distance. With 40 selected units = 1,600 distance comparisons per move order. Also, pathfinding_grid.gd get_formation_targets() uses an array with O(n) membership check in a spiral search. Use a Set for O(1) membership check and consider Hungarian algorithm or sorted greedy approach for slot assignment.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:18Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-27T15:33:18Z","labels":["algorithm","performance","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.7","title":"Generate Town Center placeholder sprite","description":"Generate an AI art placeholder sprite for the Town Center building.

Specifications (per ADR-008 sprite scale contract):
- Size: 384x256px max (3x3 footprint)
- Style: Isometric, matches existing placeholder buildings in assets/sprites/buildings/placeholder/
- Player color: Include magenta (#FF00FF) mask region for runtime shader recoloring

States to generate:
- Complete: Main building sprite with flag/banner detail, player color banner
- Under construction: Semi-transparent wireframe/scaffold version (single frame is fine for now, construction alpha is handled by code)

Reference existing placeholders (barracks.png, library.png, etc.) — they are simple colored isometric diamonds with subtle detail lines. TC should be visually larger and more prominent as the central building.

Prompt for AI art generation:
"Isometric Town Center building for a 2D RTS game, 384x256px, top-down 2:1 diamond perspective, stone and wood construction, prominent flag or banner, warm earth tones, clean pixel art style, transparent background"

Output:
- assets/sprites/buildings/placeholder/town_center.png

Acceptance criteria:
- Sprite is 384x256px or smaller
- Isometric perspective matches existing building sprites
- Visually distinct as the central/most important building
- Magenta mask region present for player color
- PNG with transparent background","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:05Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:05Z","labels":["art","buildings","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.1","title":"Adjust starting resources to cover TC construction","description":"Update resource_config.json so players start with exactly enough to build a Town Center.

Current Normal starting resources: 200 Food, 200 Wood, 100 Stone, 100 Gold
TC cost: 275 Wood, 100 Stone

New Normal starting resources: 200 Food, 275 Wood, 100 Stone, 0 Gold
Scale other difficulties proportionally:
- Easy: 300 Food, 350 Wood, 150 Stone, 0 Gold
- Hard: 150 Food, 275 Wood, 100 Stone, 0 Gold
- Expert: 100 Food, 275 Wood, 100 Stone, 0 Gold

Rationale: Gold is not needed for the initial TC or early economy (houses/farms cost only wood). Food is kept for training the first villager after TC completes. Wood and Stone are set to exact TC cost — the player must gather before building anything else.

Acceptance criteria:
- resource_config.json updated with new starting amounts
- All difficulty tiers provide at least 275 Wood + 100 Stone (TC cost)
- Gold starts at 0 across all difficulties
- Existing tests updated to reflect new values","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:03Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:03Z","labels":["data","resources","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.2","title":"Remove pre-built TC from game bootstrapper","description":"Modify game_bootstrapper.gd to stop spawning a pre-built Town Center for both player and AI at game start.

Current behavior:
- setup_demo_entities() creates a fully built TC (hp=2400, under_construction=false) for player 0
- _create_ai_town_center() creates a fully built TC for player 1
- Camera centers on the TC position

New behavior:
- setup_demo_entities() spawns only resource nodes (no player TC)
- _create_ai_town_center() removed or gutted (AI TC handled by AI build order)
- Villagers spawn at the starting position without a TC nearby
- Camera centers on the villager cluster

Acceptance criteria:
- No pre-built TC exists at game start for either player
- Villagers spawn at starting positions as before
- Resource nodes spawn as before
- Camera centers on player villagers
- Game does not crash on startup without a TC
- Victory manager handles missing TCs gracefully (no immediate defeat)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:09Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:09Z","labels":["bootstrapper","buildings","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.6","title":"Update victory manager for nomadic start","description":"Victory manager registers Town Centers during setup_victory() and uses them for conquest victory detection. With no starting TC, this needs adjustment.

Current behavior:
- setup_victory() iterates buildings looking for completed town_centers
- Conquest victory triggers when all of a player's TCs are destroyed
- No starting TC means the player could be immediately "defeated"

New behavior:
- Victory manager does not check conquest conditions until at least one TC has been built per player
- Under-construction TCs count as "player has TC intent" (not defeated yet)
- Once a player's first TC completes, normal conquest rules apply
- If a player's TC is destroyed before completion (e.g., enemy rush), they lose

Implementation:
- Add a grace period or "has_built_tc" flag per player
- construction_complete signal from buildings should register TCs with victory manager
- Conquest check skips players who haven't completed their first TC yet

Acceptance criteria:
- No immediate defeat at game start (no TC exists yet)
- Under-construction TC prevents conquest defeat
- Completed TC registers with victory manager normally
- Destroying an enemy's only TC (after completion) triggers conquest victory
- Save/load preserves the "has built TC" state","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:04Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:04Z","labels":["buildings","story","victory"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.5","title":"Handle no-drop-off gathering edge case","description":"GathererComponent currently assumes a drop-off building exists when transitioning to MOVING_TO_DROP_OFF state. With the nomadic start, there may be no drop-off until the TC is built.

Current behavior:
- When carry capacity is full, gatherer scans for nearest drop-off
- If no drop-off found, behavior is undefined (potential null reference or hang)

New behavior:
- If no drop-off exists when villager's carry is full, villager idles with resources held
- Periodically re-check for a drop-off (e.g., every 2-3 seconds)
- Once a drop-off becomes available (TC finishes construction), resume deposit
- If villager is ordered to gather before TC exists, they gather until full then wait

Acceptance criteria:
- No crash or hang when gathering without a drop-off building
- Villager holds resources and waits when no drop-off available
- Villager auto-deposits once a drop-off building completes
- Existing gathering behavior unchanged when drop-off exists","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:59Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:59Z","labels":["gathering","resources","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-a7v","title":"Update asset validator with footprint-based dimension categories","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T15:07:24Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T15:07:24Z","labels":["art","tools"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.3","title":"Auto-place TC construction site at spawn","description":"At game start, automatically place a Town Center construction site at the player's starting position. Deduct resources and assign all starting villagers to build it.

This avoids forcing the player to manually place their first building before learning the UI. The TC site appears immediately with villagers walking toward it.

Implementation:
- In game_bootstrapper or prototype_main, after spawning villagers:
  1. Call building_placer to place a TC at the starting position (or create construction site directly)
  2. Deduct 275 Wood + 100 Stone via ResourceManager.spend()
  3. Mark footprint cells as solid in pathfinder
  4. Auto-assign all starting villagers to build the site (reuse game_flow_controller.on_building_placed logic)
- The TC appears as a semi-transparent construction site (construction_alpha: 0.4) with progress bar

Acceptance criteria:
- TC construction site appears at game start at the starting position
- Starting resources are deducted (player starts with 0 Wood, 0 Stone after placement)
- All 5 starting villagers auto-walk to the site and begin building
- Construction progress is visible (progress bar, alpha)
- TC completes and becomes functional (drop-off, production queue, garrison)
- Pathfinder cells are marked solid for the TC footprint","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:50Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:50Z","labels":["bootstrapper","buildings","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.4","title":"AI nomadic start — build TC as first priority","description":"Update AI economy to handle starting without a pre-built Town Center. AI should build its TC as the very first action.

Current behavior:
- AI starts with a pre-built TC created by _create_ai_town_center()
- AI build order assumes TC already exists for unit production

New behavior:
- AI starts with only villagers (no TC)
- AI auto-places TC construction site at its starting position (mirror player logic)
- All AI starting villagers assigned to build the TC
- AI build order proceeds normally after TC completes
- AI cannot train units or execute build orders until TC is finished

Implementation:
- Reuse the same auto-place logic from story 3 for AI player
- AI economy tick should detect "no TC" state and not attempt production/gathering
- Once TC completes, normal AI behavior resumes

Acceptance criteria:
- AI starts without a TC
- AI places and builds TC as first action
- AI villagers work on TC construction
- AI resumes normal economy/military after TC completes
- AI difficulty multipliers still apply to build speed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:57Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:19:57Z","labels":["ai","buildings","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.10","title":"Update save/load for nomadic start state","description":"Ensure a game saved during the nomadic start phase (before any TC is complete) round-trips correctly through save/load.

Scenarios to handle:
- Save during TC construction (villagers building, progress partial)
- Save with villagers holding undeposited resources (no drop-off yet)

Acceptance criteria:
- Game saved during TC construction loads correctly
- TC construction progress preserved
- Villager build assignments restored after load
- Victory manager grace state preserved (no false defeat on load)
- Villagers holding resources resume waiting/depositing correctly after load","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:32Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T14:20:32Z","labels":["save-load","story"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-jev.11","title":"Integration test: nomadic start to build TC to gather wood","description":"End-to-end integration test verifying the complete nomadic start flow.

Test sequence:
1. Game starts — no pre-built TC exists
2. TC construction site is auto-placed at starting position
3. Starting resources deducted (275 Wood, 100 Stone)
4. All 5 starting villagers move to TC site and begin building
5. TC construction completes (fast-forward game time)
6. TC registers as drop-off point
7. Villager is ordered to gather wood from nearby tree
8. Villager chops tree, carries wood to TC, deposits
9. Player wood resource increases

Acceptance criteria:
- Test runs in headless mode via GdUnit4
- Covers the full flow from game start to first resource deposit
- Verifies resource deduction on TC placement
- Verifies TC completion triggers production queue attachment
- Verifies wood gathering and deposit at completed TC

{"id":"roots-of-reason-a15","title":"Tool: in-game screenshot capture for Claude Code","description":"## Problem

Claude Code cannot observe the running game. When debugging visual issues (z-ordering, sprite rendering, unit positioning), the user must manually take screenshots and save them for Claude to review. This breaks the feedback loop and slows iteration.

The existing `tools/screenshot_helper.gd` only captures static screenshots at startup — it cannot interact with a running game instance.

## Goal

A tool that lets Claude Code capture a screenshot of the running Godot game on demand, so it can observe the visual state during an active play session. This enables a tighter debug loop: launch game → perform action → screenshot → analyze → iterate.

## Design

### Architecture: GDScript HTTP server inside the game

Add a lightweight HTTP debug server that runs inside the game process (dev builds only). Claude Code sends HTTP requests to capture screenshots and (optionally) perform basic game actions.

### Components

**1. `scripts/debug/debug_server.gd` — In-game HTTP server (autoload, dev-only)**
- Listens on `localhost:9222` (configurable)
- Only active when launched with `--debug-server` user arg or `OS.is_debug_build()`
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /screenshot` — captures viewport, returns PNG bytes (or saves to path and returns JSON with path)
  - `GET /status` — returns JSON: game tick, player resources, unit count, selected units, camera position
  - `POST /command` — execute simple game actions: select-all, right-click at grid position, pan camera, set game speed
- Minimal footprint — no external dependencies, uses Godot's built-in `TCPServer`/`HTTPServer` or `StreamPeerTCP`

**2. `tools/ror screenshot` — CLI wrapper**
- Sends `GET /screenshot` to the debug server
- Saves PNG to `tests/screenshots/<name>.png`
- Returns the file path so Claude Code can `Read` it
- Usage: `./tools/ror screenshot [--output path] [--name label]`

**3. `tools/ror game-status` — Game state query**
- Sends `GET /status` to the debug server
- Returns JSON summary of current game state

**4. `tools/ror game-cmd` — Send commands to running game**
- `./tools/ror game-cmd select-all`
- `./tools/ror game-cmd right-click 5,10` (grid coords)
- `./tools/ror game-cmd camera-to 5,10`
- `./tools/ror game-cmd speed 2`

**5. Update `/launch` skill** to enable the debug server automatically

### Security

- Bind to `127.0.0.1` only (localhost)
- Only active in debug builds or when explicitly enabled via cmdline arg
- No file system write access beyond screenshot output directory

### Integration with Claude Code

After this tool ships, the workflow becomes:
1. `/launch` — starts game with debug server enabled
2. `./tools/ror screenshot --name before_fix` — Claude reads the PNG
3. Make code changes
4. `./tools/ror game-cmd select-all && ./tools/ror game-cmd right-click 5,10`
5. Wait a few seconds
6. `./tools/ror screenshot --name after_fix` — Claude verifies the fix visually

### Stories (suggested breakdown)

1. **Debug HTTP server** — TCP listener + `/screenshot` endpoint
2. **`/status` endpoint** — game state query
3. **`/command` endpoint** — basic remote control (select, right-click, camera)
4. **CLI wrappers** — `ror screenshot`, `ror game-status`, `ror game-cmd`
5. **`/launch` integration** — auto-enable debug server, update skill","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:47:34Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T22:47:34Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-a15.1","title":"Debug HTTP server with /screenshot endpoint","description":"Create `scripts/debug/debug_server.gd` — a lightweight TCP server autoload that listens on localhost:9222 and serves a `/screenshot` endpoint.

## Acceptance criteria

- GDScript autoload registered in project.godot
- Only activates when `--debug-server` is in `OS.get_cmdline_user_args()` (no-op otherwise)
- Binds to `127.0.0.1:9222` only
- `GET /screenshot` captures the viewport and returns raw PNG bytes with `Content-Type: image/png`
- `GET /ping` returns 200 OK (health check)
- Handles concurrent connections gracefully (reject if busy)
- Minimal `_process` polling — check for connections each frame only when server is active
- Tests: unit tests for request parsing and response formatting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:49:19Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T22:49:19Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-162","title":"Fix command panel not visible when villager selected","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T22:58:32Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T22:58:32Z","labels":["bugfix","ui"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6s9.1","title":"Entity query endpoint (GET /entities)","description":"Add GET /entities endpoint to the debug server that returns structured data about all game entities.

## Query parameters
- category — filter by entity_category (resource_node, unit, building)
- type — filter by resource_type or unit_type (tree, fish, villager, etc.)
- owner — filter by owner_id (0=player, 1=AI, -1=gaia)

## Response fields per entity
- name, entity_category, position (grid and screen), owner_id
- For units: unit_type, current action/state (idle, gathering, moving, combat), gather_state, gather_type, carried_amount, carry_capacity, selected, hp
- For resources: resource_type, resource_name, current_yield, total_yield, grid_position
- For buildings: building_type, hp, is_drop_off, drop_off_types

## CLI wrapper
- \`./tools/ror entities [--category X] [--type Y] [--owner N]\`
- Pretty-print table or JSON output

This is the highest-impact addition — enables Claude Code to inspect game state beyond raw pixel screenshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:18Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:18Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6s9.2","title":"Conditional wait CLI (ror wait-until)","description":"Add \`./tools/ror wait-until <condition> [--timeout N]\` that polls GET /status until a condition is met.

## Conditions
- Resource comparisons: \`wood>250\`, \`food>=100\`
- Time: \`time>120\`
- Unit state: \`idle>3\` (more than 3 idle units)

## Behavior
- Polls every 1s (configurable with --interval)
- Default timeout 60s
- Exits 0 on success, 1 on timeout
- Prints final status on exit

Enables scripted waits between game-cmd actions without manual polling.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:19Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:19Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6s9.3","title":"Scenario runner (ror scenario)","description":"Add \`./tools/ror scenario <script>\` that executes a semicolon-separated sequence of debug commands with built-in waits.

## Syntax
- Commands: select-all, gather X Y, right-click X Y, camera-to X Y, speed N, screenshot NAME, wait CONDITION
- Example: \`select-all; gather 5 4; wait wood>250; screenshot drop-off-complete\`

## Features
- Sequential execution with error handling (stop on first failure)
- Optional --file flag to read from a .scenario file
- Print step-by-step progress

Enables reproducible multi-step test sequences for visual bug verification.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:20Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:20Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6s9.4","title":"Spawn and teleport commands","description":"Add spawn and teleport actions to POST /command endpoint.

## Actions
- spawn: create entity at grid position
  - \`{"action": "spawn", "type": "villager", "grid_x": 5, "grid_y": 5, "owner": 0}\`
  - \`{"action": "spawn", "type": "tree", "grid_x": 8, "grid_y": 8}\`
  - Support: villager, tree, stone, gold, fish, and any unit/resource type in data/
- teleport: move selected units to grid position instantly (no pathfinding)
  - \`{"action": "teleport", "grid_x": 5, "grid_y": 5}\`

## CLI
- \`./tools/ror game-cmd spawn type=villager grid_x=5 grid_y=5 owner=0\`
- \`./tools/ror game-cmd teleport grid_x=5 grid_y=5\`

Enables setting up specific test scenarios without relying on random map generation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:20Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:20Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-6s9.5","title":"Annotated screenshot mode","description":"Add GET /screenshot?annotate=true that overlays debug information on the viewport capture.

## Annotations
- Entity name labels above each unit/resource/building
- Unit state text (idle, gathering wood, moving, carrying 8 wood)
- Health bars
- Grid coordinate overlay on tiles
- Selection highlight (bright outline on selected units)

## Implementation options
- Option A: Draw annotations in GDScript before capture (temporary CanvasLayer), remove after
- Option B: Post-process the PNG in Python (add text overlays via PIL)

Option A is more accurate (uses game coordinates). Option B is simpler and doesn't modify game state.

Solves the problem of multiple units stacked on the same tile being indistinguishable in raw screenshots.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:21Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-02-28T23:30:21Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-90j.1","title":"Add assert and assert-count commands to scenario runner","description":"Extend the 'ror scenario' command language with assertion primitives:
- 'assert <resource><op><value>' — e.g., 'assert food>=100', fails scenario with non-zero exit if false
- 'assert-count <entity-filter> <op> <value>' — e.g., 'assert-count units owner=0 category=villager >= 5'
- 'assert-no-idle' — all player-0 units should have a non-idle action
- 'assert-building-at <name> <x> <y>' — verify a building exists at grid position
- 'assert-entity-hp <name-or-filter> <op> <value>' — HP-based assertions
On failure: print the assertion, actual value, and expected value, then exit 1.
This is the critical enabler for turning .scenario files into integration tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-01T01:33:48Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T01:33:48Z","labels":["testing","tooling"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-nea","title":"Fog of war should not cover own buildings/units with \u003e0 health","description":"Player's own buildings and units with \u003e0 HP should never be obscured by fog of war. Acceptance criteria: (1) All buildings belonging to the player with hp \u003e 0 are always visible (no fog overlay). (2) All units belonging to the player with hp \u003e 0 are always visible (no fog overlay). (3) Fog still correctly hides enemy units/buildings outside vision range. (4) Fog still correctly covers unexplored terrain.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T03:25:36Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T03:25:36Z"}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-iya.2","title":"Add /combat-log endpoint with damage events and engagement timeline","description":"Add a debug server endpoint GET /combat-log that returns recent combat events:
- Every damage event: attacker, target, raw damage, armor reduction, final damage, remaining HP
- Overkill waste (damage dealt to already-dead units)
- DPS actual vs theoretical
- Engagement timeline (who attacked whom, when)
Requires adding a combat event emitter in the damage resolution code (prototype_unit.gd take_damage / attack logic). Store events in a capped ring buffer (last ~200 events). Add 'ror combat-log' CLI command.
This is critical for verifying that rock-paper-scissors balance invariants hold in practice.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-01T01:33:16Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T01:33:16Z","labels":["combat","debug","tooling"]}

- Idle villager count and idle duration

- Villager allocation breakdown (count per resource type + builders)
Add corresponding 'ror economy' CLI command that pretty-prints the output.

old_value:
{"id":"roots-of-reason-ylw.9","title":"Add full game loop integration smoke test","description":"Beyond the existing scene smoke test (loads every .tscn), add an integration test that actually runs the game loop. This catches crashes, NaN propagation, and cross-system interaction bugs that file-loading alone cannot detect.

Test design:
1. Boot prototype_main.tscn programmatically
2. Advance 30 seconds of game time via direct _process() calls
3. Assert no NaN values in resource stockpiles
4. Assert no orphaned nodes or error logs
5. Save full game state
6. Load game state
7. Advance 30 more seconds
8. Assert game is still in a valid state (no crashes, resources non-negative, unit counts stable)

Acceptance criteria:
- Single test file, runs in <10s headless
- Catches crashes and NaN propagation in the main game loop
- Save/load mid-run is stable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T02:35:28Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T02:35:28Z","labels":["coverage","integration"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-de0","title":"ADR-023: Debug Console & Integration Test Architecture","description":"## Context

The game needs developer tooling for: (a) manually reproducing and debugging gameplay issues, (b) fast-forwarding to late-game scenarios without playing through early game, (c) fine-grained integration tests that set up complex multi-system scenarios. Currently, the only debug tool is DebugServer (HTTP API with /economy, /combat-log, /pathfinding, /perf endpoints). This works for data inspection but cannot mutate game state.

## Decision

### Architecture: In-Game Debug Console + Scriptable Test API

**Two complementary interfaces, one shared backend:**

1. **DebugConsole (in-game overlay)** — for manual interactive debugging
   - Toggle with backtick/tilde key (\`)
   - Translucent overlay at top of screen, does not pause game
   - Text input with command history (up/down arrows)
   - Auto-complete for commands, unit types, building types, tech names
   - Output log area shows command results and errors

2. **DebugAPI (GDScript API)** — for automated integration tests
   - Static methods callable from test scripts: DebugAPI.spawn_unit(), DebugAPI.set_resources(), etc.
   - Same command implementations as console, different entry point
   - No UI dependency — works in headless test runner

3. **DebugCommandRegistry (shared backend)** — command parsing and execution
   - Commands registered as Dictionary entries: {name, args_spec, handler_callable, help_text}
   - Each command validates args, executes against live game state, returns result string
   - All mutations go through existing system APIs (GameManager, ResourceManager, etc.) — no direct state hacking

### Command Categories

| Category | Commands | Notes |
|----------|----------|-------|
| **Spawn** | spawn \<unit_type\> [player] [count] [x,y], build \<building_type\> [player] [x,y] | Uses existing unit/building creation paths. Bypasses cost checks. |
| **Control** | move \<id\> \<x,y\>, attack \<id\> \<target\>, gather \<id\> \<resource\>, set-hp \<id\> \<hp\>, kill \<id\>, teleport \<id\> \<x,y\> | Issues commands through CommandHandler (same path as player input). |
| **Economy** | give \<resource\> \<amount\> [player], give-all \<amount\>, set-pop-cap \<amount\> | Mutates via ResourceManager/PopulationManager. |
| **Tech** | research \<tech_id\>, research-all, advance-age [target_age], set-age \<age\> | Uses TechManager.complete_research() to properly trigger effects. |
| **Vision** | reveal-map, fog on/off, show-ai | Toggles VisibilityManager state. |
| **Time** | speed \<multiplier\>, pause, step [ticks], skip \<seconds\> | Modifies Engine.time_scale or GameManager.game_speed. |
| **Overlays** | show pathfinding/los/fog/spatial-index/attack-ranges, hide \<overlay\> | Renders debug visualization layers on game world. |
| **Query** | list units [player], list buildings [player], info \<id\>, stats | Read-only inspection. |

### File Organization

- autoloads/debug_console.gd — DebugConsole autoload (UI + command input)
- scripts/debug/debug_api.gd — DebugAPI static class (test-facing)
- scripts/debug/debug_command_registry.gd — Shared command registry + execution
- scripts/debug/debug_overlays.gd — Visual debug overlay rendering
- tests/helpers/scenario_builder.gd — Test helper wrapping DebugAPI for scenario setup

### Release Build Behavior

- **Debug console is STRIPPED from release exports.** Use Godot's feature tags: all debug files wrapped in \`if OS.is_debug_build()\` guards.
- DebugConsole autoload registered conditionally in project.godot (or via a debug-only scene)
- DebugAPI remains available in debug builds only — tests never run against release builds
- No debug command should have side effects that could affect game balance if somehow invoked in release

### Integration with Existing DebugServer

- DebugServer (HTTP API) continues to serve read-only endpoints
- New write endpoints added to DebugServer that delegate to DebugCommandRegistry: POST /spawn, POST /research, etc.
- This enables external tool integration (e.g., a web dashboard that controls the game)

### ScenarioBuilder Test Helper

ScenarioBuilder wraps DebugAPI for readable test setup:
\`\`\`gdscript
var scenario = ScenarioBuilder.new()
scenario.set_civ(1, "rome").set_civ(2, "polynesia")
scenario.set_age(1, "medieval").set_age(2, "medieval")
scenario.give_resources(1, {food=1000, gold=500})
scenario.spawn_units(1, "infantry", 10, Vector2i(20, 20))
scenario.spawn_units(2, "archer", 10, Vector2i(25, 20))
scenario.build(1, "barracks", Vector2i(18, 20))
# Now assert combat outcomes...
\`\`\`

## Consequences

- Two entry points (console + API) share one implementation — no logic duplication
- Commands go through existing system APIs — debug tools stay correct as systems evolve
- Stripped from release — zero performance/security impact on shipped game
- ScenarioBuilder enables readable, maintainable integration tests
- HTTP integration enables future external tooling (web debug dashboard, CI smoke tests)
- Command registry is extensible — new systems register their own debug commands","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T17:08:15Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T17:08:15Z","labels":["adr","architecture","debug","testing"]}

{"id":"roots-of-reason-kif","title":"ADR-022: Civilization Expansion — From 3 to 7 Civilizations","description":"## Context

ADR-004 established 3 launch civilizations (Mesopotamia, Rome, Polynesia) with distinct playstyles: builder, military, naval. The game is expanding to 7 civilizations. New additions must maintain the strategic triangle balance, offer genuinely different gameplay, and not create dominant strategies that invalidate existing civs.

## Decision

### Why These 4 Civilizations

**Selection criteria:** Each new civ must (a) fill an unoccupied strategic niche, (b) have rich historical identity for the Stone→Singularity timeline, (c) not duplicate an existing civ's core bonus category.

| New Civ | Niche | Justification |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| **Egypt** | Wonder/Monument Builder | Stone gathering + building HP bonuses create a defensive/wonder rush alternative to Mesopotamia's speed-focused building. Egypt trades build speed for durability. |
| **China** | Research Accelerator | +20% research speed is the only civ directly bonusing the Singularity win condition. High risk/high reward — faster tech but no military/economy bonus. |
| **Vikings** | Raid/Hybrid | Infantry attack + naval speed creates aggressive early-mid game pressure. Only civ that's strong on both land and water simultaneously. |
| **Maya** | Knowledge Economy | -15% age advancement cost + Knowledge gen creates the fastest age progression. Weak military identity (short-range unique unit) — trades combat for speed. |

### Balance Framework — 7-Civ Matrix

**Core constraint:** No civ should have >55% win rate vs any other civ in equal-skill matchups.

**Playstyle taxonomy (each civ occupies exactly one primary + one secondary):**
- Economy: Mesopotamia (primary), Egypt (secondary)
- Military: Rome (primary), Vikings (secondary)
- Naval: Polynesia (primary), Vikings (secondary)
- Research: China (primary), Maya (secondary)
- Defense: Egypt (primary), Rome (secondary)
- Aggression: Vikings (primary), Polynesia (secondary)

**Balance levers per civ (tunable in JSON, never hardcoded):**
- Passive bonus magnitude (e.g., +15% build speed)
- Unique unit stat delta from base unit (e.g., Berserker HP vs Infantry HP)
- Unique building economic advantage (e.g., Ziggurat's 0.75/s vs Library's 0.5/s)
- Unique tech power level and age availability

**Anti-dominance rules:**
1. No civ gets bonuses in more than 2 categories (e.g., Vikings: military + naval, NOT military + naval + economy)
2. Every unique unit must lose to at least one standard counter unit (e.g., Berserker still loses to massed Archers)
3. Research-boosting civs (China, Maya) must not reduce Singularity victory time by more than 20% vs a generic civ at equal play
4. No unique ability can be strictly better than the base unit it replaces — there must be a tradeoff (e.g., Chu-Ko-Nu: faster fire but lower per-hit damage)

### Unique Ability Safety Analysis

| Ability | Risk | Mitigation |
|---------|------|------------|
| Berserker rage (+50% attack at <30% HP) | Could snowball in large battles | Rage is per-unit, not army-wide. At <30% HP, unit dies in 1-2 more hits. Net DPS increase is ~15% averaged over full combat, not 50%. |
| China +20% research speed | Singularity rush too fast | Research speed bonus is additive with other bonuses, not multiplicative. At Singularity Age (5.0x multiplier), 20% base bonus becomes relatively smaller. Testing target: China Singularity victory at most 3 minutes faster than generic civ. |
| Maya -15% age cost | Age rushing to unlock late techs early | Cost reduction applies to resource cost only, not prerequisite techs. Still must research all prereqs. Testing target: Maya reaches Singularity Age at most 2 minutes faster. |
| Observatory map reveal | Free intel eliminates fog advantage | Radius limited to 15 tiles (vs full map). Does not reveal moving units, only terrain. Opponent can still surprise from outside radius. |
| War Chariot ranged+melee | Overpowered hybrid unit | Lower HP than pure Cavalry, shorter range than Archer. Jack of all trades, master of none. Costs more than either base unit. |

## Consequences

- 7 civs provides enough variety for replayability without ballooning art/test scope beyond feasibility
- Each civ has a clear strategic identity visible from the selection screen
- Balance is tunable via JSON — no code changes needed to adjust civ strength
- Balance audit bead (combat matchup test suite in debug epic) validates anti-dominance rules in CI
- Future civ additions (post-launch) should follow this same framework and pass the same balance checks","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"davetashner@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-01T17:07:44Z","created_by":"Dave Tashner","updated_at":"2026-03-01T17:07:44Z","labels":["adr","architecture","balance","civilization"]}

Created inspect_fbx.py and test_render_fbx.py utility scripts. Render output at blender/renders/archer_fbx_test_s.png.

{"description":"Replace the static villager build menu in command_panel with a dynamic, paginated list that only shows buildings the player has currently unlocked. 

**Filtering:** Buildings should be filtered by age_required, required_techs, and required_buildings gates. Locked buildings should be hidden entirely (not grayed out). The menu should include ALL buildable buildings (not just the current hardcoded 10) — including endgame buildings like GPU Foundry, Transformer Lab, AGI Core, Nuclear Plant when unlocked. Affordability dimming should remain for visible-but-unaffordable buildings.

**Pagination by category:** The build menu should be paginated by building type with tab buttons:
- Civilian: house, farm, lumber camp, mining camp, town center, etc.
- Military: barracks, archery range, stable, siege workshop, castle, etc.
- Economy: market, dock, library, factory, etc.

Each page shows only unlocked buildings in that category. Categories with no unlocked buildings could be hidden or shown empty."}
